channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - techraptor.net
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for techraptor.net

item.title: Highguard Becomes Latest Multiplayer Game to Die
item.content:encoded:
Barely a month after it released, Wildlight Entertainment has announced that their raid shooter Highguard will be shut down next week on March 12th.

After recently laying off staff, Wildlight Entertainment had most of its funding reportedly pulled by Tencent as the studio struggled to retain players after its January 26th release. In their announcement Wildlight said, "Despite the passion and hard work of our team, we have not been able to build a sustainable player base to support the game long term." They elaborate that over 2 million players had tried Highguard, and that servers will remain open until March 12th.

Additionally, there will be one final update released today or tomorrow for players to "enjoy in the remaining life of the game." This new update will include a new Warden, a new weapon, account level progression, and skill trees. The patch notes are forthcoming today, before the release.

Created by former Respawn Interactive developers, Highguard burst onto the scene at The Game Awards 2025 as the final announcement done to close out the show. Lack of communication over the next month led to many theories until the game launched, where it quickly grabbed players interested in seeing what it was about, hitting a peak of over 97k players at launch. It lacked the ability to retain it, and rumors of its death quickly struck following layoffs, and when its website shut down a couple weeks ago for a limited time.

The borrowed life was not for long, however, as Highguard will be dead inside of 2 months of its release. This is not the shortest lifespan for a live service game in recent memory, but it will join mentions with titles like Concord on the dangers of releasing live service games, and how difficult it is for those games to survive. Lacking the parent company like Concord had, at this point there is no announced plans for refunds.

A longtime lover of speculative fiction, in almost all its forms, Don joined TechRaptor in 2014 on a whim sending in an application as he was looking for… More about Don



https://techraptor.net/gaming/news/highguard-becomes-latest-multiplayer-game-to-die
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Free League's New Campaign, Vaesen City of My Nightmares, Contains Gothic Murder Mystery
item.content:encoded:
Free League Publishing has announced a new book for their Nordic horror TTRPG, Vaesen. The new book, Vaesen City of My Nightmares, introduces new lore for the setting and a compelling murder mystery in a Gothic city.

The Vaesen City of My Nightmares Announcement

According to the press release, Vaesen City of My Nightmares is a four-part prewritten campaign. The campaign has investigators uncovering a series of gruesome murders in a Gothic metropolis. Those murders are tied to a deeper conspiracy tied to the origins of The Society, the secret organization that guides investigator's through Vaesen's hidden world.

For fans of the world of Vaesen, this story hook is potent. Not much is known about The Society, despite them being a central part of Vaesen's world. The game just assumes the players' investigators are part of the organization after all. As for the Gothic city, it is inspired by 19th Century Stockholm, but can be interpreted into other cities like London and Prague.

The campaign is written by Kiku Pukk Härenstam (Song of a Falling Star) and will feature artwork by Johan Egerkrans (Vaesen The Lost Mountain Saga) and Anton Vitus. The book will include a full-color map and full-sized newspaper handout intended to enhance investigation and immersion.

Vaesen City of My Nightmares is the first Vaesen book set in an urban setting. Prior campaigns have included The Lost Mountain Saga, and the Mythic Britain and Ireland supplement. It's a notable departure from the setting's Mythic North, which is heavily steeped in Scandinavian folk tales. 

Vaesen City of My Nightmares is available for pre-order now on Free League's webstore. The book is being sold at an MSRP of $47.30 and is expected to ship on April 7. If you pre-order the book, you will receive a PDF of the book at no additional charge.

Ever since he was small, Tyler Chancey has had a deep, abiding love for video games and a tendency to think and overanalyze everything he enjoyed. This… More about Tyler



https://techraptor.net/tabletop/news/free-leagues-new-campaign-vaesen-city-of-my-nightmares-contains-gothic-murder-mystery
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
